refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall minimize energy consumption per ton moved to meet or exceed the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the high-level FR into a measurable and actionable sub-FR focused on minimizing energy per ton moved aligns with the acceptance criteria and enables targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure total energy used and tons moved over the evaluation period and verify energy_per_ton ≤ E_target.
sub_frs: Minimize energy consumption of vehicle operations
Optimize task scheduling to reduce energy usage
Monitor and report energy efficiency KPI

candidate_dps: Real-time energy monitoring and reporting system
selected_dp.selected_dp: Combination of energy-efficient vehicle powertrain design, intelligent task scheduling algorithm, and real-time energy monitoring and reporting system

potential_sources: Interaction between vehicle powertrain and task scheduling
Data flow between monitoring system and vehicle/task modules
coupling_check.notes: The selected DPs are logically coupled but can be modularized with clear interfaces to minimize complexity.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate energy consumption sensors
Risk of suboptimal scheduling algorithms impacting energy efficiency
Risk of integration complexity between vehicle and software modules
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Further refine task scheduling DP to reduce coupling with vehicle control systems.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system integration including vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services modules

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Used for real-time energy and operational data exchange between Vehicle and NGHE Solution

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives task schedules from Teleoperation module to optimize energy usage

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Ensures energy efficiency operations comply with safety policies

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports communication among modules for coordination and data sharing

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of energy efficiency logic and integration; moderate diffusion due to cross-module data sharing
Contains powertrain DP but requires data from scheduling and monitoring modules
Implements task scheduling DP; moderate diffusion due to interaction with vehicle and NGHE Solution
Limited containment related to energy efficiency; mainly safety alerts

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing energy efficiency logic and integration in NGHE Solution module minimizes diffusion and improves ownership clarity.
containment_risks: Potential complexity in NGHE Solution module due to integration responsibilities
Risk of interface overload causing maintenance challenges
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Consider decomposing NGHE Solution module if integration complexity grows beyond manageable levels.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FRs are well-defined and measurable; selected DPs logically satisfy the FRs with manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Cross-module data sharing between Vehicle and Teleoperation may increase diffusion
Integration complexity in NGHE Solution module
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores indicate good modularization with NGHE Solution as primary owner.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data and energy consumption measurements across modules.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of FRs to DPs and IMs with explicit interfaces supports traceability.

recommended_revisions: Refine task scheduling DP to reduce coupling with vehicle control
Monitor integration complexity in NGHE Solution module for potential decomposition

decision.justification: While the decomposition is sound, refinement of task scheduling DP and monitoring integration complexity is needed to reduce coupling and diffusion risks.
next_level_sub_frs: Minimize energy consumption of vehicle operations
Optimize task scheduling to reduce energy usage
Monitor and report energy efficiency KPI

revise.why: To reduce coupling between task scheduling and vehicle control and improve modularity.

assumptions: Accurate energy consumption sensors are available
Task scheduling algorithms can be optimized
open_risks: Integration complexity in NGHE Solution module
Potential suboptimal energy savings due to scheduling inefficiencies
evidence_needed: Performance data of task scheduling algorithms
Energy consumption measurements during integrated operation
